input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A static black background fills the screen with white text centered in the upper half. The top line reads "Picture frame" in a bold sans-serif font. Directly below, the second line says "from CD" in the same style. No other visual elements, movements, or changes occur within this two-second window.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A black screen displays the white text "Picture frame from CD". [2-12] The scene cuts to a static close-up of a circular picture frame resting on a wooden table. The frame and camera remain motionless throughout the duration. [12-16] A circular frame decorated with pink floral paper and white lace trim remains stationary on a wooden table. A blue compact disc enters the view from the bottom left. [16-20] Two hands hold the blue disc in the foreground, displaying a cruise ship on the ocean under a cloudy sky. Text reading "2011 CRUISE VACATIONS" and "Disney Cruise Line" is visible. The disc obscures the floral frame behind it. [20-22] The hands rotate the disc, flipping it to display the shiny, reflective silver surface on the reverse side. [22-24] The disc is flipped back over, revealing the printed blue side with the ship image again in front of the stationary frame. [30-34] A left hand adorned with silver rings grips the left edge of a standing floral circle with lace

The frame tilts slightly forward against a cluttered crafting background. The right hand then shifts back toward the stand, touching and adjusting the bottom rectangular flap of the stand as the frame tilts slightly.

Two hands hold a circular frame with a pinkish-brown patterned back and white lace trim. Initially, the right hand adjusts a light pink paper stand attached centrally. The left hand maintains a grip on the left lace edge throughout. The right hand carefully aligns the white cutout against the bottom of the central white shape on the frame, adjusting its position near the bottom edge. The background remains a cluttered crafting table with scissors and supplies visible.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A black screen displays the white text "Picture frame from CD". [2-12] The scene cuts to a static close-up of a circular picture frame resting on a wooden table. The frame features a pink floral design with a central white cutout and is bordered by white lace trim. Background elements include a white package, papers, and a black stand. The frame and camera remain motionless throughout the duration.

[12-16] A circular frame decorated with pink floral paper and white lace trim remains stationary on a wooden table. A blue compact disc enters the view from the bottom left. [16-20] Two hands hold the blue disc in the foreground, displaying a cruise ship on the ocean under a cloudy sky. Text reading "2011 CRUISE VACATIONS" and "Disney Cruise Line" is visible. The disc obscures the floral frame behind it. [20-22] The hands rotate the disc, flipping it to display the shiny, reflective silver surface on the reverse side. [22-24] The disc is flipped back over, revealing the printed blue side with the ship image again in front of the stationary frame.

recent_thinks: [24-26] The blue compact disc is placed onto the wooden table at the bottom left, removing it from the foreground. This reveals the full view of the circular floral picture frame standing centrally. A hand is partially visible in the bottom right corner.

[26-28] A hand adorned with a silver ring enters from the left, reaching toward the blue compact disc lying on the table. The central floral picture frame remains stationary, and background objects including the black stand and papers stay in their previous positions without movement.

[28-30] A right hand enters the frame from the bottom right, reaching towards and touching the white lace trim on the side of the standing floral circle. The left hand, wearing a silver ring, stays near the blue disc on the table's left side.

[30-32] A left hand adorned with silver rings grips the left edge of the standing floral circle. The hand tilts the circular object slightly to the left, altering its angle relative to the camera. The blue disc remains partially visible on the table to the left, and the background objects stay static.

[32-34] A left hand wearing silver rings grips the side of the floral circle, tilting the object further to the left to reveal its thin edge. The blue disc rests on the table surface nearby. Background items, including the black stand and papers, remain stationary while the hand adjusts the frame's angle.

[34-36] A left hand wearing a ring holds a paper pad labeled "COLORBOK" in the foreground, displaying printed photos and floral designs. The circular floral frame is moved to the background on the left. The camera view shifts to focus closely on the "COLORBOK" text at the top edge of the paper pad.

[36-38] The camera angle shifts to display the text "Pattern Pack" and "Rosewood" on the white border of a paper pad in the foreground. Behind this pad, the circular floral frame with lace trim stands on the wooden table, positioned near a black stand and other crafting supplies.

[38-40] The camera zooms in on the paper pad, bringing the floral pattern into close view. The text "Pattern Pack" and "Rosewood" is visible at the top right edge. Large pink roses with green leaves dominate the paper surface. The circular frame in the background becomes blurred as the camera focuses on the paper details.

queries.question: What is the primary color of the floral pattern on the circular decorative frame? A. Blue B. Pink C. White D. Black

samples.output: <think>The camera angle shifts slightly, keeping the "Pattern Pack Rosewood" paper pad with pink floral designs in the foreground. The circular frame with lace trim stands in the background near the black stand. A dark, blurry object moves into view on the far right edge.</think><answer></answer>

Optical disc with blue sky artwork and a white cruise ship

samples.output: <think>Two hands hold a circular frame with a pinkish-brown back and white lace trim. A light pink paper stand is attached centrally. The right hand moves away from the stand to the right, while the left hand holds the frame steady over the wooden table surface.</think><answer></answer>

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A black screen displays the white text "Picture frame from CD". [2-12] The scene cuts to a static close-up of a circular picture frame resting on a wooden table. The frame features a pink floral design with a central white cutout and is bordered by white lace trim. Background elements include a white package, papers, and a black stand. The frame and camera remain motionless throughout the duration. [12-16] A circular frame decorated with pink floral paper and white lace trim remains stationary on a wooden table. A blue compact disc enters the view from the bottom left. [16-20] Two hands hold the blue disc in the foreground, displaying a cruise ship on the ocean under a cloudy sky. Text reading "2011 CRUISE VACATIONS" and "Disney Cruise Line" is visible. The disc obscures the floral frame behind it. [20-22] The hands rotate the disc, flipping it to display the shiny, reflective silver surface on the reverse side. [22-24] The disc is flipped back over, revealing the printed blue side with the ship image again in front of the stationary frame. [30-34] A left hand adorned with silver rings grips the left edge of a standing floral circle with lace

From 80s to 88s, two hands hold a circular frame featuring a pinkish-brown back and white lace trim. A light pink folded paper stand is attached centrally to the back. The right hand returns to touch and adjust the bottom rectangular flap of the stand, while the left hand supports the frame's edge over a wooden table surface. The right hand continues to manipulate the stand's flap, adjusting its angle and causing the frame to tilt slightly against a backdrop of crafting supplies. Towards the end of the sequence, the right hand moves from the bottom flap to touch the upper triangular section of the stand, adjusting its position while the left hand maintains support on the left edge.

[88-96] Two hands hold a circular frame featuring a pinkish-brown back and white lace trim with a central light pink paper stand. The right hand shifts from touching the upper triangular section of the stand to hovering near the bottom right edge of the lace trim, then touches the lace along the right edge while the left hand supports the opposite side. The frame tilts slightly forward against a cluttered crafting background. The right hand then shifts back toward the stand, touching and adjusting the bottom rectangular flap of the stand as the frame tilts slightly.

Two hands hold a circular frame with a pinkish-brown patterned back and white lace trim. Initially, the right hand adjusts a light pink paper stand attached centrally. Then, the right hand brings a white, decorative label-shaped paper cutout into view, positioning it in front of the lower section of the frame. The left hand maintains a grip on the left lace edge throughout. The right hand carefully aligns the white cutout against the bottom of the central white shape on the frame, adjusting its position near the bottom edge. The background remains a cluttered crafting table with scissors and supplies visible.

Two hands hold a white, decorative label-shaped paper cutout in front of a circular frame featuring pinkish-brown paper, white lace trim, and a similar central white shape. From 106-108s, the hands adjust the loose cutout's alignment against the frame backdrop on a cluttered table. Between 108-110s, the right index finger touches the center of the loose white piece, positioning it slightly below the frame's central cutout. From 110-114s, the right hand shifts from touching the center to gripping the cutout's right edge, maintaining its position slightly lower than the frame's attached piece amidst crafting supplies like scissors.

recent_thinks: [24-26] The blue compact disc is placed onto the wooden table at the bottom left, removing it from the foreground. This reveals the full view of the circular floral picture frame standing centrally. A hand is partially visible in the bottom right corner.

[26-28] A hand adorned with a silver ring enters from the left, reaching toward the blue compact disc lying on the table. The central floral picture frame remains stationary, and background objects including the black stand and papers stay in their previous positions without movement.

[28-30] A right hand enters the frame from the bottom right, reaching towards and touching the white lace trim on the side of the standing floral circle. The left hand, wearing a silver ring, stays near the blue disc on the table's left side.

[114-116] Two hands hold a white decorative paper cutout in front of a circular frame featuring a similar attached shape. The right hand moves from gripping the edge to pointing a finger at the center surface of the loose white piece, while the background remains a cluttered wooden table with crafting supplies visible.

The frame and camera remain motionless throughout the duration. [12-16] A circular frame decorated with pink floral paper and white lace trim remains stationary on a wooden table. A blue compact disc enters the view from the bottom left. [16-20] Two hands hold the blue disc in the foreground, displaying a cruise ship on the ocean under a cloudy sky. Text reading "2011 CRUISE VACATIONS" and "Disney Cruise Line" is visible. The disc obscures the floral frame behind it. [20-22] The hands rotate the disc, flipping it to display the shiny, reflective silver surface on the reverse side. [22-24] The disc is flipped back over, revealing the printed blue side with the ship image again in front of the stationary frame. [30-34] A left hand adorned with silver rings grips the left edge of a standing floral circle with lace

Two hands hold a white, decorative label-shaped paper cutout in front of a circular frame featuring pinkish-brown paper, white lace trim, and a similar central white shape. From 106-108s, the hands adjust the loose cutout's alignment against the frame backdrop on a cluttered table. Between 108-110s, the right index finger touches the center of the loose white piece, positioning it slightly below the frame's central cutout. From 110-114s, the right hand shifts from touching the center to gripping the cutout's right edge, maintaining its position slightly lower than the frame's attached piece amidst crafting supplies like scissors.

The right hand is positioned near the right side of the frame, having released the loose paper. The floral-patterned frame remains stationary in the center of the cluttered workspace. The left hand continues to point at the lace trim while the right hand holds the right edge of the frame. The white cutout remains lying flat on the wooden table directly beneath the frame.

[130-140] A left hand adorned with silver rings points at and touches the white lace trim on the left edge of a circular floral frame standing upright on a wooden table. Initially, the right hand releases the frame and exits the view. A white paper cutout lies flat and undisturbed on the table surface beneath the frame. Throughout the segment, the left index finger maintains contact with the lace, occasionally moving slightly downwards along the trim. The frame remains stationary, and the background shows cluttered crafting supplies.
